question_text: Now let me ask you about a number of specific things Presdent Reagan said he wants to do. For each, tell me if you favor or oppose it. READ EACH ITEM Item: His call to cut 41.4 billion dollars from the 1982 federal budget

As it works now, Social Security payments, payments to the handicapped and to those on government pensions, and other federal benefits are indexed to rises in the cost of living. As the Consumer Price Index goes up, the amount of benefits goes up by the same percentage. Those who favor this system feel those who get the benefits should not suffer from increases in cost of living. Others feel that by increasing the amount of benefits as prices go up, government spending goes up and that makes inflation still worse. All in all, do you favor or oppose indexing these benefits to the cost of living?

Some Democrats in Congress are working on a tax bill as a substitute for the Kemp-Roth tax proposal which calls for a reduction of the federal personal income tax rate 10% each year for the next three years, which many congressmen think is too inflationary. Let me ask you if you favor or oppose some of these new tax cut proposals. Item: Increasing the amount an eligible individual can set aside for retirement in an IRA plan as a deduction against taxes from $1,500 to $10,000

Another proposed way of keeping government benefits in line with the cost of living would be to index the benefits to changes in wages. In other words, benefits would increase according to the annual average rise in wages. If, over the past few years, the benefits had been geared to wage increases rather than price rises, the government would have saved 6.5 billion dollars a year. Would you favor or oppose a new system, under which both price and wage increases would be looked at and the benefits would go up by whichever index went up least?

Some Democrats in Congress are working on a tax bill as a substitute for the Kemp-Roth tax proposal which calls for a reduction of the federal personal income tax rate 10% each year for the next three years, which many congressmen think is too inflationary. Let me ask you if you favor or oppose some of these new tax cut proposals. Item: Increasing the amount an eligible individual cans set aside for retirement in a Keogh (KEY-OH) Plan as a deduction against taxes from $7,500 to $15,000

Now let me ask you about some other tax cuts that many Congressmen of both parties favor. For each, tell me if you favor or oppose that tax cut. READ EACH ITEM Item: Increasing the amount an eligible individual can set aside for retirement in an IRA (READ AS INITIALS) plan as a deduction against taxes from $1,500 to $2,000

The Reagan Administration, through Secretary of the Interior James Watt, has announced that it wants to lease to oil companies sizable parts of the Pacific Ocean off the California coast for oil exploration. The State of California objects to this policy claiming such drilling activities will adversely affect the coastal environment there. Do you favor or oppose the leasing to oil companies those tracts off the California coast?

One of the reasons Social Security costs have gone up so much is that benefits are now indexed to go up as prices go up. If Social Security payments had been geared to wage increases rather than price rises, the government would have saved 6.5 billion dollars a year over the past few years. President Reagan is opposed to changing the current system of indexing Social Security payments. Would you favor or oppose changing the way Social Security benefits are indexed, so that both price and wage increases would be looked at and the benefits would go up by whichever index went up least?

As you know, the federal government is now running heavy deficits which could reach 100 billion dollars a year. At the same time, many people believe that more federal money is needed to properly finance Social Security and to build up our military defenses to keep ahead of the Russians. That could mean raising taxes, at least temporarily. The Republicans are considering passing, for two years only, a national sales tax on all purchases people make, except food and medicine. On each purchase you made, you would pay a 10 cent additional sales tax for every dollar you spent. Item: Would you favor or oppose a two-year national sales tax of 10 cents on every dollar on all purchases, except food and medicine, to go just to a National Defense Trust Fund to keep the U.S. ahead of the Russians and to eliminate the federal deficit?

Do you favor or oppose linking the federal income tax to the cost of living?

A year from this July 1st, another 10% cut in the federal income tax rate is scheduled to go into effect. Those who favor this additional tax cut say it will increase consumer spending and will help the economy recover. Those who oppose this tax cut say it will keep the federal deficit high, which will keep interest rates from coming down and will not bring about economic recovery. Do you think next year’s 10% income tax cut should he postponed or not?

Another tax proposal is that everyone making up to $25,000 a year pay a flat 14% of their total income with deductions allowed only for employee business expenses, home mortgage payments, charitable contributions, and state and local income and property taxes. In addition, all those who receive Social Security and veterans’ benefits would not pay taxes on that income. All interest from state and local government bonds used to finance state and local governments would be tax-free. For those making over $25,000, there would be an additional basic tax that would increase up to a total of 28% for married people with joint incomes of over $65,000. Would you favor or oppose this simplified federal income tax?

It has been proposed that the federal income tax be simplified by going to a single, across-the-board rate of 14% taxation for everyone, but at the same time eliminating nearly all the current deductions people take so that more of people’s income is taxable.  Another provision would be to double the $1,000 exemption per person, which would keep the poor from paying higher taxes. In general, do you favor or oppose this approach to changing the tax system.
